title: The public acceptance of green taxes: 2 million voters express their opinion

tax_mentions.context: Swiss referendum on three proposals for taxes on fossil energy/non-renewable fuels.
tax_mentions.notes: Main policy object; analyzed via referendum votes.

tax_mentions.context: Ballot proposals taxed fossil energy at the border on energy content.
Proposal returned revenues through lower social security contributions, and voters' support was modeled.
tax_mentions.notes: Revenue recycling is central to the acceptability analysis.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is a subsidy-using counter-proposal, not the tax instrument itself.
This is a renewable-energy subsidy proposal, not a direct fuel/carbon tax.
These are non-tax climate measures mentioned only in passing.

inclusion_reason: This paper directly analyzes referendum acceptance and vote choice for Swiss energy taxes on non-renewable/fossil fuels, including a green tax reform with revenue recycling.
